claims.evidence: alien.js marchInterval(aliveCount): pure fn reading only window.SI.Config each call, no writes; interval = clamp(ceil(48*count/55),1,48) — non-decreasing in count so non-increasing as aliveCount drops 55->1; at count=55 interval=48, at count=1 interval=1 (strictly smaller).

game.js marchGrid(): horizontal branch computes one `dx = marchDirection*ALIEN_STEP_X` and applies it in a single for-loop to every aliens[j].x — same delta for all alive aliens each step, including after resolveBulletAlienCollisions() has spliced dead aliens out of state.aliens.

game.js marchGrid(): edge-touch move sets `marchPendingDrop = true` (grid still completes that horizontal step); the very next due march step takes the `if (marchPendingDrop)` branch, adds ALIEN_ROW_STEP to every alien's y and flips `marchDirection = -marchDirection` together, then returns.

game.js: marchStepCounter incremented once per marchGrid() call (called once per SI.Game.update()), compared against SI.Alien.marchInterval(aliveCount); update(dt) never reads dt for march timing/scaling — comment confirms 'dt accepted for interface compatibility ... never scaled by dt's magnitude'.

marchGrid() and resolveBulletAlienCollisions() only ever mutate alien.x/alien.y (or splice whole aliens out of the array); no alien ever gains a class/prototype or extra march-time field — shape is stable through marching.

claims.text: Alien entities created in alien.js carry two fields beyond the claimed {x,y,width,height,points} shape: `row` (used once at creation for pointsForRow tiering, never read again) and `alive: true` (set once, never read — dead removal is done by splicing aliens out of state.aliens in resolveBulletAlienCollisions, not by flipping this flag).

marchInterval's speed ramp uses a distinct tuning ceiling (ALIEN_MARCH_MAX_INTERVAL=48) rather than the grid's total alien count (55), so the ratio math is a real ceil() rather than an identity mapping — a design choice left open by the intent.
Loop.js adds a spiral-of-death guard (delta capped at 3x fixed step) around the fixed-timestep accumulator feeding SI.Game.update(), independent of the march determinism contract.
